title: Nomad zero-root message validation bypass and replay drain

historical_exploit.attack_summary: Nomad's Replica accepted fraudulent messages because an upgrade path left the zero root acceptable in the confirmAt map, so process(bytes) could authorize messages that were not proven in the trusted message set. Attackers replayed or slightly patched exposed calldata, mostly changing recipient addresses and token/amount targets, to drain bridge-held ERC20 assets.
historical_exploit.attacker_profile: Initial exploiters plus many copycat addresses; some used private orderflow/helper contracts, then public calldata enabled broad replay by unprivileged users.
historical_exploit.victim_or_protocol_loss: More than 186 million USD in ERC20 assets were stolen from the Nomad Bridge, including WBTC, WETH, USDC, and other tokens.
preconditions: Destination-side bridge execution accepts a message whose root/proof or message-presence check can default to an acceptable value.
The message payload controls recipient, token, amount, or handler data that releases live bridge-held assets.
Replay or patched calldata can be submitted by an unprivileged caller without rebinding to a unique already-consumed source event.

value_path.how_value_was_extracted: Attackers called process(bytes) with crafted or replayed bridge messages that passed validation even though the message had not been properly proven, causing bridge handlers to transfer stored tokens to attacker-controlled recipients.
value_path.why_it_paid: The fraudulent messages targeted real token balances already held by the bridge, so each accepted message produced an immediate positive attacker balance delta and reduced the bridge's backing liquidity.

mapping.why_this_might_map: BridgeIndex has the same high-value invariant class: a destination execution must be uniquely bound to a valid source deposit through originChainId, originNonce, resourceId, dataHash, relayer votes, and proposal status before BotBridge.execute releases or mints value. Any mismatch, default-acceptance, stale proposal, replay, source-refund race, or dataHash construction gap could turn a historical Nomad-style message validation bypass into extraction of current BridgeIndex BNB/USDT ledger rows.

notes: Coinbase reports that Nomad's initial exploitation began in Ethereum block 15259101 and that process(bytes) accepted fraudulent messages through the zero-root acceptableRoot path.

For BridgeIndex, this seed should only survive scanning if a non-admin caller can make Vote/BotBridge release an existing live BNB or USDT ledger row beyond entitlement; relayer-only, admin-only, future-deposit, or no-live-balance mappings must be rejected.
Primary BridgeIndex code anchors reviewed for this mapping: BridgeIndex/src/contracts/BotBridge.sol, BridgeIndex/src/contracts/Bridge.sol, and BridgeIndex/src/contracts/Vote.sol.
